variance_note: Single wbox run (rAF-rate-limited CPU; second run not feasible in tool budget). wbox is the slowest fleet host; frame rate in headless Chrome is ~5 fps (200 ms rAF interval) because the Bobcat CPU cannot complete one tic+render faster than that.

palette.note: WebGL2: 256×1 RGB texSubImage2D when paletteDirty. Higher n than alder (63 vs 2) due to E1M1 palette flashes triggered by CDP keystrokes during slow gameplay. p99 5.3 ms is an outlier spike; p50/p90 = 0.1 ms.

upload.note: WebGL2: 320×200 R8 texSubImage2D + drawArrays. p50=0.1 ms is fast; p99=6.5 ms and max=7.3 ms reflect occasional Bobcat cache-miss spikes. Still well below the 200 ms rAF interval.

raf_interval.note: wbox is CPU-rate-limited: rAF fires at ~5 fps (200 ms mean interval) in headless Chrome because completing one doom._web_frame() + render takes ~200 ms on the Bobcat. This is a headless measurement artefact; on a real display the game would be locked to 35 Hz tic rate and rAF would fire at display rate.

raf_duration.note: Total rAF callback duration. p50=2.4 ms (vs 0.2 ms alder — 12× slower); p99=30.6 ms. Max 239 ms is a rare scheduling spike. The frame budget comparison to alder is dominated by wasm engine time, not JS overhead.

worklet.note: Same headless-Chrome limitation as alder: AudioContext did not arm without a trusted user gesture. Worklet instrumentation is implemented but cannot fire in headless mode.

input_latency.note: p50=101.6 ms ≈ half of the 200 ms rAF interval (expected: event lands at random phase within a slow frame). p99=3396.7 ms is an outlier scheduling spike on the Bobcat (slow CPU + OS preemption). Not representative of display-synced gameplay. On a real 35 Hz display, input latency would be bounded by the tic period (28.6 ms) not the headless rAF interval.
